compiled_state.title: As a plugin developer, I am prevented from accidentally adding dev-only files to the plugin directory
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
Scope: Create a pre-commit hook check-plugin-boundary.sh that reads a positive-enumeration allowlist and blocks commits adding files to plugins/dso/ that are outside the allowlist.

Hook location: plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/check-plugin-boundary.sh
Allowlist location: plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf

The allowlist uses glob patterns to enumerate all paths that are permitted inside plugins/dso/. When a commit attempts to add a file to plugins/dso/ that does not match any allowlist pattern, the hook exits non-zero and prints a descriptive error message that names both the offending file and the allowlist file.

Hook registration: Register in the git pre-commit hook framework (consistent with existing check-portability.sh, check-shim-refs.sh). Do NOT register in plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/pre-bash.sh — that is a Claude Code PreToolUse dispatcher. The boundary check must fire on git commits, not just Claude Code Bash calls.

Fail-open: If the allowlist file is missing or unreadable, the hook exits 0 and prints a warning. The allowlist absence must never block commits.

Done Definitions:
- [ ] plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/check-plugin-boundary.sh exists and is executable
- [ ] plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf exists with glob patterns covering all currently-permitted plugin paths (post-S1 state)
- [ ] The allowlist file contains a comment block explaining how to add new permitted paths
- [ ] Running the hook against the post-S1 plugin directory produces zero violations
- [ ] Attempting to commit a file at plugins/dso/docs/designs/test.md triggers the hook and prints an error naming the allowlist file
- [ ] Hook exits 0 when allowlist file is missing (fail-open)
- [ ] Hook is registered in the git pre-commit hook runner (same mechanism as check-portability.sh)
- [ ] tests/hooks/test-check-plugin-boundary.sh exists and passes: permitted path allowed, prohibited path blocked, missing allowlist passes, error message is parseable

Considerations:
- [Reliability] Fail-open is non-negotiable — a broken or missing allowlist must never block legitimate commits
- [Testing] Test covers: permitted path (existing plugin file pattern), prohibited path (designs dir), missing allowlist (fail-open), error message format (parseable for CI output)
- [Maintainability] Allowlist patterns should be broad enough to cover future legitimate plugin growth (e.g., skills/**/*.md, hooks/**/*.sh) not just current files — use glob wildcards, not exact paths

Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked — proceed unless a significant assumption could send implementation in the wrong direction; document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body:
REPLAN: Done definitions revised per corrected epic spec (brainstorm re-run 2026-04-10). The hook and allowlist must be created at .claude/hooks/pre-commit/ (NOT plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/). The existing implementation placed them in the wrong location — inside the distributed plugin directory — where the hook is a no-op in host projects. Story reopened to relocate both files and update .pre-commit-config.yaml and all test references.

Revised done definitions:
- DD1: check-plugin-boundary.sh exists at .claude/hooks/pre-commit/check-plugin-boundary.sh (not inside plugins/dso/)
- DD2: plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf exists at .claude/hooks/pre-commit/plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf (not inside plugins/dso/)
- DD3: .pre-commit-config.yaml entry references .claude/hooks/pre-commit/check-plugin-boundary.sh
- DD4: hook exits non-zero when a file outside the allowlist is staged under plugins/dso/
- DD5: A test stages plugins/dso/docs/findings/test.md and confirms hook exits non-zero with actionable error; all test references use the .claude/hooks/pre-commit/ path
- DD6: plugins/dso/scripts/run-overlay-retrospective.sh output path does not contain plugins/dso/; produces output under docs/findings/
comments.author: Test
